data.title: Yellowstone Raises Partial Offer Price for Greentech Technology Shares
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Greentech Technology International Limited ( (HK:0195) ) has issued an update.

Yellowstone International Limited, managed by Argyle Street Management, has raised its voluntary cash partial offer for up to 230 million shares of Greentech Technology International Limited from HK$0.40 to HK$0.55 per share. The revised price represents a significant premium to the last trading price and keeps all other offer terms unchanged, underscoring a stronger bid to entice existing shareholders.

At the new offer price, the total potential consideration rises to HK$126.5 million, which the offeror will fund entirely from internal resources, with its financial adviser confirming sufficient liquidity. The premium of roughly 96% over the last trading day’s closing price signals a more aggressive stance by Yellowstone, potentially reshaping Greentech’s ownership structure and highlighting the company’s perceived strategic value in the market.

More about Greentech Technology International Limited

Greentech Technology International Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company whose shares are the subject of an unsolicited partial takeover offer, indicating it operates in a sector attracting strategic investment interest. Its shareholder base includes qualifying shareholders targeted by Yellowstone International Limited, which is pursuing a cash-based acquisition strategy through a managed offer structure.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$382.5M

data.title: China Shenhua Board Backs New Independent Directors in Governance-Focused Move
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Shenhua Energy Co ( (HK:1088) ) has issued an update.

China Shenhua Energy Company Limited has released an overseas regulatory announcement summarizing resolutions passed at the 20th meeting of the sixth session of its board of directors, held in Beijing on 23 July 2026. The board meeting complied with PRC company law, applicable listing rules and the company’s Articles of Association, with directors attending in person, by proxy and via video connection.

At the meeting, the board approved a proposal to nominate Yih, Dieter Lai Tak and Zhang Mengjiao as candidates for independent non-executive directors for the remainder of the current board term. The nomination committee concluded that the candidates meet legal and regulatory requirements, have relevant experience, and satisfy corporate governance standards, and the board agreed to submit the proposal to the general meeting for shareholder consideration.

The resolutions underline China Shenhua’s ongoing refreshment of its independent oversight and adherence to governance norms for listed companies in China and Hong Kong. For stakeholders, the appointment of qualified independent non-executive directors is intended to reinforce board independence, regulatory compliance and oversight quality as the company manages its large integrated energy operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1088) stock is a Hold
with a HK$36.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Shenhua Energy Co stock,
see the HK:1088 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Shenhua Energy Co

China Shenhua Energy Company Limited is a leading integrated energy company in the People’s Republic of China, listed in Hong Kong and Shanghai. The group operates across coal production, power generation, and related energy services, serving domestic and international markets as one of China’s largest coal-based energy suppliers.

The company is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive, independent non-executive and employee directors, and is subject to PRC company law and listing rules in both Hong Kong and Shanghai. Its corporate governance framework follows the Articles of Association and relevant codes for listed companies.

Average Trading Volume: 14,112,717

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$1086.2B

data.description:

An announcement from Unisound AI Technology Co., Ltd. ( (HK:9678) ) is now available.

Unisound AI Technology reported that its controlling shareholder, CEO and executive director Dr. Huang Wei bought 56,000 H shares on the open market between July 6 and July 16, 2026, for about HK$3.98 million, while executive director Dr. Liu Shengping acquired 15,140 H shares for roughly HK$1.13 million over a similar period. These insider purchases lifted Dr. Huang’s total interest to about 25.99% of issued shares and reaffirmed that the core shareholder group has not reduced its stakes since listing.

The company said the purchases, financed with personal funds, reflect management’s confidence in Unisound AI’s development prospects and growth potential, and noted that Dr. Huang plans to further increase his H shareholding subject to market conditions and regulatory constraints. The board highlighted that any future increases will be structured to avoid triggering a mandatory general offer, signaling a deliberate approach to capital markets while reinforcing stability and alignment between management and minority investors.

More about Unisound AI Technology Co., Ltd.

Unisound AI Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based company listed in Hong Kong that issues a mix of domestic unlisted shares, unlisted foreign shares and H shares. The company’s controlling shareholders include founders and related entities, who collectively hold a significant minority stake, underscoring a concentrated ownership structure and strong insider presence in its share capital.

data.description:

China Shenhua Energy Co ( (HK:1088) ) has provided an announcement.

China Shenhua Energy has proposed appointing legal veteran Yih, Dieter Lai Tak and governance specialist Zhang Mengjiao as independent non-executive directors for the sixth session of its board, subject to shareholder approval at a forthcoming general meeting. Their terms will run until the end of the current board session, reflecting the company’s effort to reinforce independent oversight and regulatory compliance in its Hong Kong-listed structure.

Incumbent independent non-executive directors Dr. Yuen Kwok Keung and Dr. Chen Hanwen will step down once the new appointments take effect, and both have confirmed there is no disagreement with the board or issues that might affect the group’s operations. A circular detailing the proposed changes will be sent to shareholders, signalling an orderly transition that aims to maintain board stability and protect stakeholder confidence in the company’s governance.

An update from China Shenhua Energy Co ( (HK:1088) ) is now available.

China Shenhua Energy Co held its second extraordinary general meeting of 2026 in Beijing, with both on-site attendance and online voting available to A-share investors, and reported that the meeting complied with Chinese securities regulations and the company’s articles of association. Shareholder turnout was high, with 4,339 shareholders and proxies representing about 73.7% of issued shares, including substantial participation from both A and H share classes.

All resolutions on the agenda were approved by wide margins, including the appointment of the company’s 2026 auditors and the annual remuneration plan for directors, each passing as ordinary resolutions with more than 99% of votes cast in favour. The company noted there were no opposing voting intentions announced in advance, no required abstentions under Hong Kong listing rules, and no new or amended resolutions, underscoring broad shareholder support for its governance and oversight arrangements.

data.title: Shanghai Seer flags strong revenue surge and narrower loss for first half of 2026
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6106) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd., a Hong Kong-listed Chinese maker of intelligent robots and robot brains, is sharpening its focus on industrial automation at home and abroad as overseas markets become a key growth engine. The company’s emphasis on software-centric control systems and its push into export markets underscores its ambition to scale beyond China’s borders and capture rising demand for warehouse and factory automation.

The group expects first-half 2026 revenue of at least RMB252.6 million, up more than 60% year on year, and gross profit of at least RMB118.3 million, at least 65% higher than the prior period. Driven by strong sales of intelligent robots, control systems and surging overseas revenue, the company forecasts a narrowed net loss of no more than RMB40.5 million and a sharply reduced adjusted net loss, reflecting improving operational efficiency and tighter cost control ahead of detailed interim results due by end-August.

More about Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based provider of intelligent robots and so-called robot brains, focusing on automation solutions with a growing overseas presence. Listed in Hong Kong as a Class H share issuer, the company targets industrial and logistics customers seeking smart robotics and software-driven control systems to enhance operational efficiency and productivity.

data.title: China Railway Signal & Communication Wins Eight Major Rail Transit Contracts
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An update from China Railway Signal & Communication ( (HK:3969) ) is now available.

China Railway Signal & Communication has secured eight significant contracts in China’s rail transit sector between May and June 2026, spanning both railway and urban metro projects. The wins include major system integration works on new mainline routes such as the Yining-Aksu Railway and the Mianyang-Suining-Neijiang Railway, as well as traction power upgrades and on-board CTCS-3 equipment for 350 km/h Fuxing trains.

In urban rail, the company will renew and upgrade signaling and video surveillance systems on key metro lines in Nanjing and Beijing, and supply the signal system for the first phase of Jinan Rail Transit Line 9. With total tender amounts in the hundreds of millions of renminbi, these awards reinforce the company’s leading role in China’s rail and metro signaling market and are likely to support future revenue and strengthen its competitive position in large-scale infrastructure projects.

More about China Railway Signal & Communication

China Railway Signal & Communication Corporation Limited is a Chinese rail transit technology company specializing in signaling, communication, train control and related system integration. It focuses on mainline railways and urban rail transit markets, providing equipment procurement, installation, commissioning and integrated solutions for high-speed, conventional and metro lines.

Average Trading Volume: 5,082,526

Microware Group Limited has extended the long stop date for completing its placing of new shares under a general mandate, after agreeing a supplemental placing agreement with its placing agent. data.title: Hengrui Wins NMPA Nod to Move Cervical Cancer Combo to First-Line Use
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1276) ) has shared an update.

Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals and its subsidiary Suzhou Suncadia Biopharmaceuticals have received approval from China’s National Medical Products Administration to market their innovative drug combo, camrelizumab for injection with famitinib malate capsules. The regimen is now cleared as a first-line treatment for patients with recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer, upgrading its prior positioning from second-line to first-line therapy in advanced disease.

The approval strengthens Hengrui’s presence in oncology, particularly in targeted immunotherapy for gynecologic cancers, and underscores the company’s ability to move pipeline assets into earlier lines of care. This shift to first-line use could expand the eligible patient pool, enhance the commercial potential of camrelizumab and famitinib, and reinforce the firm’s competitive standing in China’s fast-growing market for innovative cancer treatments.

An update from Unisound AI Technology Co., Ltd. ( (HK:9678) ) is now available.

Unisound AI Technology Co., Ltd. has announced a board-approved plan to repurchase up to HK$0.1 billion worth of H shares on the open market within six months, under an existing mandate that permits buybacks of up to 4,510,988 H shares, or 10% of its issued H-share capital. The repurchased shares may be cancelled to reduce registered capital or held as treasury stock, with the transaction funded by internal and self-raised resources in line with the company’s articles and PRC and Hong Kong regulations.

The board said the initiative is intended to signal confidence in Unisound’s business outlook and enhance shareholder value, while maintaining a sound financial position and compliance with public float requirements. Management emphasized that any buybacks will be executed at the board’s discretion and subject to market conditions, and cautioned investors that there is no assurance on the timing, scale or pricing of the repurchases, underscoring the discretionary and conditional nature of the program.

data.description:

Greentown China Holdings ( (HK:3900) ) has issued an announcement.

Greentown China Holdings has announced a major leadership reshuffle effective 23 July 2026, including the resignation of chairman and non-executive director Liu Chengyun, who also stepped down from leading the nomination, ESG and risk control committees and from his other roles in the group. Acting chief executive officer Geng Zhongqiang resigned from that role but will remain an executive director, with both executives stating they have no disagreement with the board.

The company has appointed Zhao Hui as executive director, chairman of the board, chief executive officer and authorised representative, as well as chairman of the nomination, ESG and risk control committees and a member of the remuneration committee, centralising key governance and operational responsibilities under his leadership. In addition, Jiang Feng has been appointed as an executive director and executive president, signaling a reconfigured top management team that may influence Greentown China’s strategic direction and operational oversight while aiming to ensure continuity and stability in corporate governance.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3900) stock is a Sell
with a HK$8.55 price target.

data.title: Epiworld Signals Sharp First-Half Profit Rebound on Core Business Strength
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An update from Epiworld International Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2726) ) is now available.

Epiworld International Co., Ltd. has issued a positive profit alert, indicating that it expects to swing from a net loss of about RMB2.0 million in the first half of 2025 to a net profit between RMB44.0 million and RMB54.0 million for the six months ended June 30, 2026. The company also forecasts adjusted net profit in the range of RMB134.0 million to RMB144.0 million, up from RMB97.0 million a year earlier, attributing the gains mainly to significant improvements in revenue and gross profit from its core business.

The interim figures are based on unaudited management accounts and may be subject to change before the formal results are released by the end of August 2026, prompting the company to urge shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution in trading its shares. The sharp turnaround and robust adjusted earnings signal strengthened operational performance and may enhance Epiworld’s market standing, though stakeholders must wait for the finalized numbers to fully assess the sustainability of this improvement.

More about Epiworld International Co., Ltd. Class H

Epiworld International Co., Ltd., incorporated in the PRC and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the electronics and semiconductor-related technology sector through its core business lines. The group focuses on generating revenue and gross profit from its principal electronic technology operations, positioning itself within the broader high-tech manufacturing and component supply chain.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

AK Medical Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1789) ) has issued an update.

AK Medical Holdings Ltd. has granted share options and awarded shares to a director and selected employees under its existing share option and share award schemes, aligning incentives with long-term company performance. The company issued 355,000 options, equal to about 0.03% of its share capital, at an exercise price of HK$5.84 per share, with vesting tied to both corporate and individual performance.

The options vest in two equal tranches in July 2027 and July 2028, subject to achieving defined financial metrics such as revenue, net profit, and working capital efficiency, as well as personal appraisal results. Unvested options will lapse if employment or eligibility ends, indicating a clear retention and performance-driven structure that links employee rewards to sustainable operational and financial outcomes without additional financial assistance from the company.
